title: calceolaria cordifolia
intro: Calceolaria cordifolia, also known as the heart-pouched slipper or heart flower, is a flowering plant species belonging to the Calceolaria genus. It is traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and expectorant properties. The plant's leaves, flowers, and stems are used to make herbal preparations that offer health benefits such as relieving respiratory issues, reducing fever, and soothing skin irritations. Herbal preparations of Calceolaria cordifolia can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and are often used to treat coughs, colds, and other upper respiratory infections.
